description:
Symptom: every `bl` command prints two warnings to stderr:

    warning: plugin `balls-plugin-github` describe failed: error: unrecognized subcommand 'describe'
    warning: plugin `balls-plugin-github-issues` describe failed: error: unrecognized subcommand 'describe'

Cause: balls-core src/plugin/runner.rs::Runner::describe() (introduced under bl-8b71 per the doc comment) invokes `<plugin-bin> describe` to detect native-protocol plugins per SPEC §5.2. Both sibling plugins in balls-github-plugin (crates balls-plugin-github and balls-plugin-github-issues) only expose the legacy subcommands (auth-setup/auth-check/push/sync for github, plus the issues-side equivalents) and clap rejects 'describe' as an unrecognized subcommand. Core treats the failure as 'silent fall-through to the legacy shim' per the doc comment but still leaks the clap error to stderr as a warning.

Two options:

(a) Implement the describe subcommand in both plugins. Returns the DescribeResponse the plugin would emit (projection schema, event subscriptions). This is the right direction long-term (per the SPEC §5.2 native protocol bl-8b71 introduced) and removes the warning by giving core what it asked for. Plugin-side change.

(b) Make balls-core's Runner::describe() suppress the clap 'unrecognized subcommand' error specifically. The fall-through is intentional, so the warning is misleading — it implies something's broken when nothing is. Core-side change, smaller blast radius.

Recommend (b) immediately for noise relief, and (a) as a follow-up if/when the native protocol's projections give the issues plugin meaningful upside.

Test: a sync against a legacy plugin should not produce 'describe failed' warnings on stderr.
title: plugins missing native protocol 'describe' subcommand — warning noise on every bl invocation [bl-c343]
